title: glyceria maritima var. palustris
intro: Glyceria maritima var. palustris, also known as marsh fescue, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a marsh-dwelling plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to help reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties. Glyceria maritima var. palustris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
